title: DL Profile Meta-Schema
description: Defines the structural requirements that any DL (Description Language) profile schema must satisfy. A profile schema is valid if it conforms to this meta-schema. SDL (Software Description Language) is the reference profile.

$id.description: Unique URI identifier for this profile schema.

title.description: Human-readable name for this primitive schema.

description.description: Description of what this primitive represents in the profile's domain.

type.description: All DL primitives must be JSON objects.

dl.description: DL binding metadata. Declares which DL concepts this schema implements, which Layer it belongs to, and how its Perspectives are structured.

profile.description: The name of the DL profile this schema belongs to.

version.description: The DL spec version this profile conforms to.

concepts.description: The DL meta-concepts this primitive implements. Each primitive implements one or more DL concepts.

layer.description: Which DL structural layer this primitive primarily belongs to. 'schema' = static structure (what exists). 'semantic' = dynamic behavior (what happens). 'presentation' = rendering (how it is shown).

layer_ref.description: The id of the zoom Layer this primitive belongs to, as declared in the profile's Layer registry (e.g. spec/layers.sdl.json). Allows the renderer to place this primitive at the correct zoom level. Examples: 'layer_platform', 'layer_service', 'layer_logic'.

perspectives.description: If this primitive implements the Perspective concept, list all declared perspectives. Perspective Contract: at least one perspective with role 'intent' is always required — it is the primary artifact that humans audit. A perspective with role 'implementation' is optional; it may be omitted when implementation is delegated to AI tooling. The per-Layer perspective_contract in the Layer registry governs which roles are required at each zoom level.

name.description: The perspective name as it appears in the schema.

role.description: 'intent' = plain language view for domain experts (always required). 'implementation' = technical view for engineers (optional). 'supplementary' = additional view.

required.description: Whether this perspective is required on every instance of this primitive. Defaults to true for 'intent' perspectives, false for 'implementation' and 'supplementary'.

notes.description: Optional notes about how this primitive maps to DL concepts.

DLLayer.description: A Layer definition for a DL profile's Layer registry. Defines a named zoom scope — the scale at which descriptions are written. The renderer reads the zoom chain (zooms_into / zooms_out_to) to implement generic navigation without per-layer custom logic.

id.description: Unique identifier for this Layer. Convention: 'layer_<name>' to make Layer ids visually distinct.

order.description: The zoom order of this Layer. Lower numbers are higher (more zoomed out). The outermost Layer has order 1.

description.description: What this zoom scope describes and who its primary audience is.

zooms_into.description: The id of the Layer one level below this one (more detailed). null if this is the innermost Layer.

zooms_out_to.description: The id of the Layer one level above this one (less detailed). null if this is the outermost Layer.

perspective_contract.description: Which Perspective roles are required and optional at this zoom level.

required_roles.description: Perspective roles that every primitive at this Layer must implement.

optional_roles.description: Perspective roles that primitives at this Layer may optionally implement.
